parts.name: The Avoider
parts.description: When pressure or dread builds, this part takes over with scrolling, cancelling, sinking into the couch, ordering food — converting saved or pressured time into numbing nothing.

parts.name: The Calculator
parts.description: The relentless comparing-and-scoring voice that measures the user against everyone around them and converts every social moment into data points about their standing.

parts.name: The Hungry One
parts.description: The young, embarrassed, achingly wanting part — wants to be seen as good, wants the rocket emoji, the "incredible," the specific proud, the announcement with their name in it, and is mortified to need any of it.

parts.name: The Inner Critic
parts.description: The clipped, sneering nighttime tutor-voice that dresses down every kindness, every accomplishment, every flicker of softness, replaying mistakes and warning of public exposure.

parts.name: The Polisher
parts.description: Delivers the upgraded, finished, container-shaped version of the user — to bosses, parents, friends, partners — converting messy truths into smooth surfaces before they can leak out.

parts.name: The Quiet Self
parts.description: The unrehearsed, un-performing version of the user that surfaces when nobody is watching — at the kitchen table, at the laundromat, on the couch laughing — and that the user is slowly learning to recognize and trust.

parts.name: The Witness
parts.description: The reflective awareness that names what's happening, catches the patterns mid-loop, and refuses to let the calculator's outputs become recipes — the journal-keeping voice that turns observation into information rather than instructions.

parts.name: The Calculator
parts.description: Runs rapid social and strategic math to optimize every response, weighing how each choice will be perceived by others.

parts.name: The Concealer
parts.description: Shuts down honest disclosure to keep vulnerability from getting bigger or being argued with.

parts.name: The Inner Critic
parts.description: Replays mistakes and judges every word for inadequacy.

parts.name: The Numb-Out
parts.description: Converts uncomfortable saved time into scrolling, food, and dissociation when emotion threatens to surface.

parts.name: The People-Pleaser
parts.description: Says yes reflexively to preserve belonging and avoid the discomfort of disappointing anyone.

parts.name: The Performer
parts.description: Tries to engineer the perfect impression through rehearsal and stylistic flourish, often sabotaging the actual message.

parts.name: The Quiet Self
parts.description: An emerging settled presence that arrives when performance drops, allowing softness and being-with rather than doing-for.

parts.name: The Rule-Follower
parts.description: Stays inside assigned tasks and waits for permission to avoid stepping on toes or being judged for overreach.

parts.name: The Scared Imposter
parts.description: Carries the fear of being exposed as not good enough, scrambling beneath the competent surface.

The Calculator.positiveIntent: Get through any moment where someone might be evaluating me without visibly fumbling — meetings with Drew, Sunday calls home, the elevator on the way up.
The Calculator.fearedOutcome: The two-second pause where it becomes obvious I don't actually belong. Drew's face doing the small flicker of "oh."
The Calculator.whatItProtects: The version of me that had been thinking about the project carefully all week — the one who knew the dependency thing and never got the chance to say it.
The Calculator.userNotes: Loudest on the bus to work, before any one-on-one with Drew. Rehearses opening sentences. Quietest making coffee with Avery in silence — that's the tell.

The Hungry One.positiveIntent: Be safe. Not be the one in the room who didn't know the thing, who got asked and froze.
The Hungry One.fearedOutcome: Being seen as a kid in a costume — not a real adult, not yet. Drew or Reese realizing it before I do.
The Hungry One.whatItProtects: (itself — the one the rehearsing and the scanning and the smoothing are all built around keeping out of view)
The Hungry One.userNotes: Surfaces when someone is unexpectedly kind. Reese saying "good call on the timeline thing" and wanting to crawl out of my skin. The kindness is what gets in past the others.
